posts.name: Daniel Bailey
posts.cooked: We have lots of threads about best qpr//bargain wines. How about the opposite? Name 2 wines (incl vintage) you’d want to drink now (stress now) regardless of price, one you’ve already tried and one you haven’t. For me, I’d love to drink the 90 roumier bonnes mares again and try the 1990 Roumier Musigny given how amazing the BM was.

posts.name: Tom Chen
posts.cooked:
1989 Haut Brion (within the last year) or 1988 Petrus (from a bottle bought at release). Both drank in more of a bigger group setting, but still remember how great these were and would love to re-drink in a smaller setting.
1962 La Tache. Birth year and have only had a few truly outstanding burgundies, although I think I’ve some pretty good/great ones.

posts.name: William Segui (high quality ITB)
posts.cooked: 1995 Coche CC

posts.name: Ryan Caughey
posts.cooked:
One I’ve had: 93 Rousseau Chambertin. My epiphany wine and I’ve had it a couple more times, each time sublime.

One I haven’t tried: a perfectly aged bottle of Ramonet or Lafon Monty. Maybe 86 Ramonet or 92 Lafon or something. Or a well-aged Coche CC—like a perfect 96. Mature GC white Burg is the top of the pyramid for me.

posts.name: Dav3_Dyr0ff
posts.cooked:
Tough to narrow either down to one.

Have tried - 1978 Chave Hermitage (runners-up 1982 Mouton, 1990 Latour, 1945 Latour but from a better bottle this time)

Haven’t tried - 1961 Hermitage La Chapelle (runners-up 1945 Mouton, 1945 DRC RC, or any vintage of DRC RC or Jamet Cote Brune, 1966 Palmer, 1947 Cheval or Petrus, '74 Marthas, and so many others)

posts.name: Jeff M 0 L l
posts.cooked: Give me that 69’ Chappellet from Napa

posts.name: Mark Golodetz

Best wine at a friend’s fiftieth. In fact the best I have ever tasted.

Jaboulet Hermitage La Chapelle 1961

Wine I would love to try
La Tache 1962. (TBH, I did taste at a pre Auction sample, but the bottle was dead).

posts.cooked: I’ve had the 1959 HB and it is a stunner. Would love to try it again except the 1955 was even more impressive when I tried it in the same flight.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: Would be 45, 59 and 61 Mouton. Have had several bottles of the glorious 1982. I still give the 89 Petrus the edge even though both, every time, have been 100 to me. Would love to have that Petrus again. It’s been 10 years since I had it last. Had a 98 Petrus last year that was stunning. These are gorgeous wines.

posts.name: Loren Supp
posts.cooked:
Taste: 1945 Mouton. Even if only because of the context, it sounds like tasting a piece of history. Wine’s apogee.

Taste Again: 2010 Haut Brion. Still the best wine I’ve ever tried, and I would happily drink more. Anyone want to swap for some bourbon ?

posts.name: Mark Cronmiller
posts.cooked:

Have had before - 94 Merryvale Oakvile Ink Grade (?) not even sure I got the name correct, but had this in tasting room, it was bottled without a label and wasn’t ever released as far as I was told … was an amazing wine

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
jp_trevino:

Want to try - 1990 Chave Cathelin
Want to try again - 1961 Pavie

I had that 1961 Pavie at Bern’s out of a 375. It was outstanding. I never would have a guessed it was anything older than a 1982, in fact it drank like a 1982. It was quite the score when I had it, and not terribly expensive either. This was about five or six years ago.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: The problem with 45 mouton and 45 RC is that the vast majority of bottles are fake; it’d be hard to find something with perfect provenance.

posts.name: Mike Vanone
posts.cooked:
Here are my inputs for picking only 2 wines as per OP:

2012 Screaming Eagle (because I had to pick a year and this probably has the appropriate age for a Napa Cab and was a good vintage. I’d like to try any year of SE)

2001 Chateau d’Yquem (I’ve had several other vintages of Yquem and have confirmed they are the best of Sauternes)

Edited to add: If the OP rules are to try an actual vintage of a particular wine you’ve had before you’d like to try again I’ll say a 2012 Hundred Acre Ark Vineyard. One of the best I’ve had.
